title: aethusa cynapium subsp. elata
intro: Aethusa cynapium subsp. elata, also known as tall hemlock water dropwort, is a plant species within the carrot family (Apiaceae) characterized by its tall, fern-like foliage and small white flowers. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as alleviating respiratory issues, reducing fever, and providing relief from menstrual cramps and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Aethusa cynapium subsp. elata can be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
